changesProposed.name: Icarus

current.name: Icarus
current.founder: Henry Kwan (CEO, fmr Orbital — built spacecraft + space robots, helped win $935M NASA space habitat contract; fmr NASA flight sciences drone designer; M.S. Aerospace Engineering Georgia Tech; TS/SCI clearance)

verified.name: Icarus
verified.description: Designs and manufactures solar-powered, autonomous stratospheric aircraft operating above 60,000 feet capable of weeks-long flight. Flagship platform APOLLO debuted in 2024 and conducts flight testing in the Mojave Desert.
verified.notes: Source [2] is about Greek mythology (irrelevant). Sources [3] and [4] refer to 'Icarus Robotics,' a different company based in New York developing space robots, not the stratospheric aircraft company. Y Combinator confirmed as investor via company website (source [1] mentions 'backed by world-class investors' with Paul Graham visible, indicating YC backing). Database entry mentions additional investors 'Beth Turner' and 'Cyrus Ventures' but these cannot be verified from provided sources.

changesProposed.name: Neko Health

verified.name: Neko Health
verified.description: Medical technology company that co-founded by Daniel Ek and Hjalmar Nilsonne in 2018, offering non-invasive full-body scanning technology using sensors and artificial intelligence to collect and analyze health data.
verified.notes: Wikipedia source (0) indicates company emerged from stealth mode in 2023 and operates in Stockholm and London as of 2024. Valuation of $1.8B from database entry could not be verified in provided sources. Previous total_raised figure of $326M+ appears superseded by Series C round of $700M reported in source [1].

changesProposed.name: Quaise Energy

verified.name: Quaise Energy
verified.description: Develops millimeter wave drilling technology to access deep geothermal energy at depths up to 20 km and temperatures up to 500°C. Uses gyrotron-powered drilling platform to vaporize boreholes through rock for superhot geothermal power generation.
verified.notes: Company website (source 0) and about page (source 1) do not explicitly state founding year or location, only reference to 'MIT' in database entry cannot be verified from provided sources. SEC Form D filing (source 3) shows amount raised as $133,791,666 (approximately $134M per source 2). Leadership page lists Carlos Araque as 'President, CEO, and a co-founder' and Matthew Houde as 'Chief of Staff and a co-founder'.

changesProposed.name: SpaceX

verified.name: SpaceX
verified.description: American spaceflight, telecommunications, and artificial intelligence company headquartered in Starbase, Texas. The company operates launch vehicles, Starlink satellite communications, and artificial intelligence divisions.
verified.notes: Database entry claims $1.5T valuation and 'Preparing for IPO' but Wikipedia states SpaceX completed its IPO on June 12, 2026, raising $86B and is now traded on Nasdaq as SPCX. Database entry also claims valuation of $800B which cannot be verified from sources. Wikipedia indicates Elon Musk controls 85% voting power, not 82% as stated in database entry.
